text: Meeting a deadline can be stressful, but good time management helps. Start by breaking a large project into smaller tasks and setting mini-deadlines for each one. Prioritization is also key—decide which tasks are most important or urgent and focus on those first. If you realize the original scope is too big, manage it by adjusting your goals or asking for an extension early, not at the last minute. Throughout the process, communicate regularly with your team or supervisor; let them know your progress and any challenges. This builds trust and often leads to helpful support, making it much easier to finish on time.
